edges.description: CHD1 dysfunction perturbs chromatin remodeling and epigenetic state.
Epigenetic dysregulation disrupts normal neurodevelopmental trajectories.
CHD1 disruption can impair cranial neural crest and jaw cartilage development in model systems.
edges.target: Cranial neural crest and jaw cartilage disruption

edges.description: Altered chromatin regulation contributes to downstream neurodevelopmental dysfunction.
Altered neurodevelopment contributes to autism phenotype.
Neurodevelopmental dysfunction contributes to speech motor planning deficits.
Cranial neural crest and cartilage disruption may contribute to dysmorphic craniofacial morphology.
edges.source: Cranial neural crest and jaw cartilage disruption

nodes.description: Facial dysmorphic features are part of the syndrome gestalt.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability
evidence.snippet: Here we describe six CHD1 heterozygous missense variants in a cohort of patients with autism, speech apraxia, developmental delay and facial dysmorphic features.

nodes.description: CHD1 missense dysfunction is associated in cultured fibroblasts with increased levels of the closed chromatin mark H3K27me3, supporting but not alone proving an epigenetic disease mechanism.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability
evidence.snippet: Finally, we demonstrate increased levels of the closed chromatin modification H3K27me3 in fibroblasts from a subject carrying a de novo variant in CHD1.

nodes.description: Autism is a frequent neurobehavioral feature in the original CHD1 missense cohort.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability
evidence.snippet: Here we describe six CHD1 heterozygous missense variants in a cohort of patients with autism, speech apraxia, developmental delay and facial dysmorphic features.

nodes.description: Behavioral counseling and autism-focused supports should be individualized for social communication and behavioral needs.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability.
evidence.snippet: Three of these patients also received a diagnosis of autism, although one (Subject 1) no longer carries this diagnosis.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability
evidence.snippet: Our results suggest that variants in CHD1 can lead to diverse phenotypic outcomes; however, the neurodevelopmental phenotype appears to be limited to patients with missense variants, which is compatible with a dominant negative mechanism of disease.

evidence.reference_title: A Saudi Girl With Co-occurring CHD1 (Pilarowski-Bjornsson Syndrome) and ASH1L Gene Variants.
evidence.snippet: Notably, this case is unique due to the coexisting presence of an absent, small, and homeotic disks protein 1 homolog like a histone lysine methyltransferase (ASH1L) variant and developmental dissociation.

nodes.description: Pilarowski-Bjornsson syndrome is caused by heterozygous CHD1 missense variants affecting a chromatin remodeler required for normal epigenetic regulation.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability
evidence.snippet: Here we describe six CHD1 heterozygous missense variants in a cohort of patients with autism, speech apraxia, developmental delay and facial dysmorphic features.

nodes.description: Reduced Chd1 function in Xenopus disrupts cranial neural crest development and jaw cartilage morphology, providing model-organism support for craniofacial features in CHD1-related disease.
nodes.id: Cranial neural crest and jaw cartilage disruption

evidence.reference_title: Using an aquatic model, Xenopus laevis, to uncover the role of chromodomain 1 in craniofacial disorders.
evidence.snippet: Further analysis indicated that embryos deficient in Chd1 had defects in cranial neural crest development and jaw cartilage morphology.

nodes.description: Developmental, occupational, and physical-therapy supports should be individualized for developmental delay, intellectual disability, and hypotonia.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability.
evidence.snippet: All five patients had developmental delay and hypotonia, all are female and some had epileptiform abnormalities on an EEG (Table 1).

nodes.description: Developmental delay is one of the core presenting features of Pilarowski-Bjornsson syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability
evidence.snippet: Here we describe six CHD1 heterozygous missense variants in a cohort of patients with autism, speech apraxia, developmental delay and facial dysmorphic features.

nodes.description: Hypotonia was reported across the clinically characterized founding patients.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability.
evidence.snippet: All five patients had developmental delay and hypotonia, all are female and some had epileptiform abnormalities on an EEG (Table 1).

nodes.description: Intellectual disability is included in the founding summary of the CHD1-related neurodevelopmental disorder.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability.
evidence.snippet: In summary, our data show that missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with a novel neurodevelopmental disorder with intellectual disability, autism, seizures, speech apraxia, and dysmorphic features.

nodes.description: Downstream neurodevelopmental dysfunction produces the core autism, speech, and developmental phenotype of Pilarowski-Bjornsson syndrome.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability
evidence.snippet: Here we describe six CHD1 heterozygous missense variants in a cohort of patients with autism, speech apraxia, developmental delay and facial dysmorphic features.

nodes.description: Seizures are reported in the founding CHD1-associated neurodevelopmental disorder summary.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability.
evidence.snippet: In summary, our data show that missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with a novel neurodevelopmental disorder with intellectual disability, autism, seizures, speech apraxia, and dysmorphic features.

nodes.description: Neurology evaluation and seizure-directed pharmacotherapy should be guided by clinical seizure history and EEG findings where present.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability.
evidence.snippet: In summary, our data show that missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with a novel neurodevelopmental disorder with intellectual disability, autism, seizures, speech apraxia, and dysmorphic features.

nodes.description: Speech apraxia is a distinctive and recurring neurodevelopmental feature.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability
evidence.snippet: Here we describe six CHD1 heterozygous missense variants in a cohort of patients with autism, speech apraxia, developmental delay and facial dysmorphic features.

nodes.description: Speech-language therapy is relevant for speech apraxia and related communication needs.

evidence.reference_title: Missense variants in the chromatin remodeler CHD1 are associated with neurodevelopmental disability.
evidence.snippet: Four of our subjects with CHD1 mutations carried a diagnosis of speech apraxia.
